definition: a fixed regular payment for work
hook: Your paycheck used to come in a different form entirely.
modernAnchor: A salary lands in your account every two weeks. You barely look at it.
journey: Roman soldiers received an allowance specifically for buying salt. Salt preserved food and was traded like currency.
That allowance was called the salarium. From sal, the Latin word for salt.
A good soldier was worth his salt. A bad one wasn't. The phrase still exists today.
When your paycheck arrives, you are technically being paid in salt money.
